questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Hawaii real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Hawaii requires salesperson candidates to be at least 18 years old. Additional requirements include a high school diploma, 60 hours of pre-license education, passing the exam with 70%, and a background check.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a Hawaii salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Hawaii requires 60 hours of approved pre-license education for salesperson candidates. After completing the coursework, candidates must pass the licensing exam with a minimum score of 70%.

questions.question_text: A salesperson candidate scores 68% on the Hawaii licensing exam. What is the result?
questions.correct_answer: The candidate fails and must retake it
distractors: The candidate may appeal the score
The candidate receives a provisional license
questions.explanation: The passing score for the Hawaii salesperson exam is 70%. A score of 68% does not meet the threshold, so the candidate must retake the examination.

questions.question_text: After passing the salesperson exam, a new licensee must work under the supervision of whom?
questions.explanation: All Hawaii salespersons must work under the direction and supervision of a licensed broker. A salesperson cannot practice independently or supervise other salespersons.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is required for a Hawaii salesperson license application?
questions.explanation: Hawaii salesperson applicants must undergo a criminal background check as part of the licensing process. Other requirements include being 18, having a high school diploma, completing 60 hours of education, and passing the exam.

questions.question_text: How many hours of broker pre-license education does Hawaii require?
questions.explanation: Hawaii broker candidates must complete 80 hours of approved broker education. They must also have an active salesperson license and 3 years of full-time experience within the prior 5 years.

questions.question_text: A salesperson has been licensed for 2 years and works 40 hours per week. Can they apply for a broker license?
questions.correct_answer: No, 3 years of experience is required
distractors: Yes, if the broker endorses them
No, 5 years of experience is required
questions.explanation: Hawaii requires broker candidates to have 3 years of full-time (40 hours/week) experience as an active salesperson within the prior 5 years. Two years does not meet this threshold.

questions.question_text: A salesperson earned an Experience Certificate for broker licensing. How long is it valid?
questions.explanation: The Experience Certificate, which verifies that a salesperson has met the 3-year full-time experience requirement, is valid for 2 years from the date of issuance.

questions.question_text: What minimum passing score must a broker candidate achieve on the Hawaii broker exam?
questions.explanation: Hawaii broker candidates must score at least 75% on the broker licensing exam, which is higher than the 70% required for salesperson candidates.

questions.question_text: A broker candidate worked part-time (20 hrs/wk) for 6 years. Does this qualify as full-time experience?
questions.correct_answer: No, 40 hours per week is required
distractors: Yes, total hours exceed the minimum
Yes, if verified by the broker
questions.explanation: Hawaii defines full-time experience as 40 hours per week. Part-time hours are not aggregated to meet the requirement. The candidate needs 3 years at 40 hours per week within the prior 5 years.

questions.question_text: How many total continuing education hours must a Hawaii licensee complete per biennium?
questions.explanation: Hawaii licensees must complete 20 hours of CE per biennium: 6 hours of core education (3 hours Part A and 3 hours Part B) plus 14 hours of elective courses.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes 14 elective CE hours but skips the core courses. Can they renew?
questions.correct_answer: No, 6 core hours are also required
distractors: Yes, if they pay a late fee
questions.explanation: Hawaii requires both 6 hours of core CE (Part A: 3 hours, Part B: 3 hours) and 14 hours of electives for a total of 20 hours. Core courses cannot be skipped or replaced with electives.

questions.question_text: Hawaii real estate licenses expire on which date?
questions.explanation: All Hawaii real estate licenses expire on December 31 of even-numbered years. The renewal deadline is November 30 for pocket IDs, with a final deadline of December 31.

questions.question_text: A licensee fails to renew by December 31 of an even-numbered year. What happens on January 1?
distractors: The license converts to inactive status
questions.explanation: If a Hawaii licensee fails to renew by December 31, the license is automatically forfeited on January 1 of the following odd-numbered year. There is no grace period.

questions.question_text: A broker licensed in California moves to Hawaii. Does California licensure transfer automatically?
questions.correct_answer: No, Hawaii has no blanket reciprocity
questions.explanation: Hawaii does not offer blanket reciprocity with any state. Out-of-state licensees must meet Hawaii's education, examination, and experience requirements to obtain a Hawaii license.
